sector_specific_levers.lever_name: GST Input Tax Credit Optimisation & Recovery
condition_precedents: GST reconciliation between GSTR-2A / 2B and purchase register completed
Reverse charge mechanism (RCM) liabilities identified and accounted
ERP configured for auto-population of GST fields and ITC matching
industry_sources: GST Act 2017 Section 17(5) — blocked credit categories (statutory)
DPE — Survey of CPSEs 2022-23 (dpe.gov.in/reports/annual-report) — GST compliance benchmarks for CPSEs
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Reconcile GSTR-2A/2B (supplier invoices filed) vs GSTR-3B (ITC claimed) for all returns periods; identify unclaimed ITC by GST head
Investigate ITC gaps: misclassified blocked credits, supplier non-filing, and wrong GST head mapping in ERP; quantify recoverable amount
File DRC-03 or revised GSTR-3B for recoverable credits; engage with vendors who have not filed returns to resolve mismatch
Implement monthly auto-reconciliation in ERP/GSTN portal; update chart of accounts to correctly classify ITC-eligible expenses

diagnostic_signals.signal: ITC claimed as % of total GST paid <80% for input-intensive sectors (manufacturing, construction, logistics) indicating under-claim
diagnostic_signals.confirms: ITC recovery will yield direct cash refund or credit

diagnostic_signals.signal: GSTR-2A/2B vs GSTR-3B ITC mismatch >5% of eligible ITC pool
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Reconciliation exercise will identify recoverable amount

diagnostic_signals.signal: Blocked credit (Schedule II items) incorrectly claimed or ITC-eligible items incorrectly coded in ERP as non-ITC
diagnostic_signals.confirms: GL reclassification will unlock additional ITC

required_data_fields: GSTR-2A/2B data vs GSTR-3B ITC claim (all periods under review)
GL coding for input costs by GST head (ITC eligible vs blocked)
Vendor GSTIN compliance status and filing history
ERP GST configuration and tax code mapping
Prior-year tax authority ITC mismatch notices if any

sector_specific_levers.lever_name: Ind AS 116 Lease Reclassification (Operating vs. Finance)
condition_precedents: Lease register compiled with all lease contracts (term, payments, break clauses)
Incremental borrowing rate (IBR) confirmed with treasury / auditors
Ind AS 116 accounting entries reviewed by statutory auditors
industry_sources: MCA — Indian Accounting Standard Ind AS 116 Leases (mca.gov.in) — statutory accounting standard
ICAI — Ind AS 116 Implementation Guidance (icai.org) — practical expedients for short-term and low-value leases
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Build comprehensive lease register: identify all contracts that qualify as leases under Ind AS 116 (embedded leases in service contracts included)
Calculate right-of-use (ROU) asset and lease liability for each lease using incremental borrowing rate; validate against existing balance sheet
Configure ERP (SAP RE-FX or Oracle Lease) for automated lease amortisation, interest accrual, and disclosure schedule generation
File revised balance sheet with ROU assets; update debt covenants disclosure and DPE reporting; train finance team on ongoing Ind AS 116 accounting

diagnostic_signals.signal: Lease-related disclosures incomplete or absent in financial statements for entity with >₹50 Cr annual lease payments
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Ind AS 116 compliance gap creates regulatory and audit risk

diagnostic_signals.signal: No structured lease register—lease data maintained in spreadsheets without systematic contract tracking
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Lease register implementation is prerequisite for compliance

diagnostic_signals.signal: Embedded leases in service contracts not identified, creating under-disclosure risk
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Comprehensive contract review will ensure full compliance scope is captured

required_data_fields: All lease contracts (property, vehicles, equipment) with commencement date, term, and lease payments
Service contracts requiring embedded lease assessment
Incremental borrowing rate by tenure for lease liability discounting
ERP lease accounting module capability
DPE reporting requirements for lease disclosures

condition_precedents: CSR programme impact assessment completed (outcome per rupee spent)
Preferred implementing agency panel approved by CSR committee
CSR spend aligned to Section 135 and Schedule VII permissible activities
industry_sources: MCA — CSR Annual Reports & Impact Data (mca.gov.in/content/mca/global/en/acts-rules/ebooks/circulars.html) — Companies Act 2013 Section 135 CSR spending data
DPE — Survey of CPSEs CSR Expenditure 2022-23 (dpe.gov.in/reports/annual-report) — CPSE CSR spend impact benchmarks
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Audit CSR spend against Companies Act mandate (2% of average 3-year net profit): classify by project type, implementing agency, and impact measurement quality
Rationalise implementing agencies: reduce from >10 to <5 strategic partners with stronger impact monitoring and lower admin overhead
Standardise CSR project selection on mandate-compliant outcomes with measurable beneficiary impact (MoU-linked deliverables)
Track CSR compliance ratio, project impact metrics, and admin overhead as % of CSR spend annually

diagnostic_signals.signal: CSR admin and overhead (implementing agency fees + internal management) >15% of total CSR spend
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Rationalising implementing agencies will increase direct beneficiary impact

diagnostic_signals.signal: CSR spend not fully compliant with mandate—unspent amounts or spend on non-eligible activities
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Compliance review will prevent penalty under Companies Act Section 135

diagnostic_signals.signal: Implementing agency performance not monitored against outcome metrics for >50% of CSR projects
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Strengthening monitoring will improve impact and partner accountability

required_data_fields: CSR spend by project, implementing agency, and activity type
Companies Act mandate calculation (2% of average 3-year net profit)
Implementing agency contracts and performance data
Impact measurement reports by project
CSR committee composition and decision-making process

sector_specific_levers.lever_name: Public Procurement Reform (GeM / DGS&D Rate Contracts)
condition_precedents: GeM portal adoption rate mapped for eligible categories
DGS&D / RCCL rate contracts reviewed for applicable categories
CVC / CAG audit risk assessment for procurement reforms completed
industry_sources: GeM — Government e-Marketplace Annual Report 2023-24 (gem.gov.in/resources/files/Annual_Report_GEM.pdf) — GeM procurement savings benchmarks for CPSEs
CVC — Procurement Reform Guidelines (cvc.gov.in) — public procurement best practices
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Audit procurement compliance: calculate GeM portal adoption %, DPP/DAC mandates for defence/PSU, and proportion of single-vendor awards
Develop GeM onboarding plan for categories eligible under GFR Rule 149 / GFR 2017; train procurement team on GeM ordering process
Implement rate-contract framework for high-frequency categories (stationery, IT peripherals, fuel, security) via tender or GeM RC
Track GeM adoption %, single-vendor award ratio, and savings vs benchmark prices monthly; report to Board/CVC

diagnostic_signals.signal: GeM portal adoption <60% of eligible procurement categories despite GFR mandate for PSUs
diagnostic_signals.confirms: GeM adoption will reduce procurement cycle time and ensure compliance

diagnostic_signals.signal: Single-vendor award (limited tender or nomination) >30% of procurement value without documented justification
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Open tendering or rate contracts will reduce procurement cost and audit risk

diagnostic_signals.signal: Procurement unit prices above corresponding DGS&D/GeM rate contract for same specification
diagnostic_signals.confirms: GeM or rate contract adoption will immediately reduce unit prices

required_data_fields: Procurement spend by category with current sourcing method (GeM, tender, nomination)
GeM portal eligibility assessment by category
DGS&D/GeM rate contract prices for common items
CVC audit observations on procurement compliance
GFR 2017 requirements applicable to the entity

condition_precedents: MRO spend classified and vendor rationalisation target set
Annual rate contracts (ARCs) negotiated with empanelled MRO vendors
FSNL / safety stock norms reset based on consumption data
industry_sources: DPE — Survey of CPSEs 2022-23 (dpe.gov.in/reports/annual-report) — MRO and maintenance spend benchmarks for CPSEs
Hackett Group — World-Class Procurement Benchmarks 2024 (thehackettgroup.com/research/) — MRO consolidation savings 8-20%
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Aggregate MRO spend from plant stores, purchase orders, and P-cards across all sites; classify by category (mechanical, electrical, instrumentation, safety)
Identify consolidation candidates: categories with >5 vendors and fragmented spend; build RFQ for preferred vendor agreements by category
Negotiate VMI (Vendor Managed Inventory) agreements for fast-moving C-class items with top MRO distributors; implement consignment for critical spares
Build MRO catalogue in procurement system; enforce catalogue buying; track MRO cost per site and spend under management monthly

diagnostic_signals.signal: MRO vendor count >100 per site with top-20 vendors representing <50% of spend, indicating fragmentation
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Consolidation will improve leverage and reduce procurement overhead

diagnostic_signals.signal: MRO spend outside purchase order (P-card or credit card) >20%, indicating maverick purchasing
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Catalogue discipline and VMI will reduce off-contract buying

diagnostic_signals.signal: MRO unit price variance >30% for same item across sites due to decentralised purchasing
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Group contracts will harmonise pricing across plants

required_data_fields: MRO spend by site, category, and vendor (12 months)
Purchase order vs credit card spend split
Price data for common items across sites
Current VMI and consignment arrangements
Critical vs non-critical spares classification

sector_specific_levers.lever_name: BEE Energy Audit & PAT Cycle Compliance
condition_precedents: Bureau of Energy Efficiency (BEE) audit completed
Perform, Achieve, Trade (PAT) cycle targets identified
Energy conservation measures (ECMs) approved by board
industry_sources: BEE — Perform Achieve Trade (PAT) Scheme Cycle Results (beeindia.gov.in/content/pat-cycle) — energy savings achieved by designated consumers including CPSEs
CEA — Annual Report 2023-24 (cea.nic.in/annual-report/) — industrial energy efficiency benchmarks
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Commission BEE-certified energy audit for all facilities above PAT cycle threshold; establish SEC (Specific Energy Consumption) baseline by production unit
Prioritise energy efficiency projects by payback period (<3 years): motor replacement, variable frequency drives, compressed air, and lighting retrofit
Execute top-10 projects with investment approval; file PAT cycle designation certificate; install DECs (Designated Energy Consumers) as required
Track SEC monthly vs PAT cycle target; submit BEE annual report; prepare for ESCerts trading if target exceeded

diagnostic_signals.signal: Specific Energy Consumption (SEC) above BEE benchmark for the sector or above PAT cycle target
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Energy efficiency projects will reduce SEC and electricity cost, and avoid PAT penalty

diagnostic_signals.signal: Energy cost as % of COGS >12% for electricity-intensive processes (metal, cement, chemical, glass)
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Energy efficiency investment will materially reduce production cost

diagnostic_signals.signal: Motor load factor <60% for >30% of installed motor capacity indicating oversized or inefficient motors
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Motor replacement or VFD installation will reduce electricity consumption

required_data_fields: Energy consumption data by fuel type, unit, and process (12 months)
BEE PAT cycle designation status and SEC target
Energy audit report with top-10 efficiency recommendations and investment estimates
Electricity tariff structure and peak demand charges
Motor inventory with load factor and age data

condition_precedents: Voluntary Retirement Scheme (VRS) designed per DPE guidelines
DPE / Ministry approval obtained for headcount reduction plan
Roles earmarked for automation mapped and implementation timeline set
industry_sources: DPE — Survey of CPSEs 2022-23 (dpe.gov.in/reports/annual-report) — employee cost as % of revenue benchmarks for CPSEs
CAG — Audit Reports on CPSEs (cag.gov.in/en/report) — manpower productivity findings
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Conduct workforce audit: map headcount by function, grade, and age profile; compare span of control and employee-to-revenue ratio vs DPE benchmark
Design VRS (Voluntary Retirement Scheme) package compliant with DPE guidelines; model savings vs payoff cost over 5 years
Launch VRS with targeted eligibility criteria (age ≥50, non-critical skills); process applications with DPE approval as required
Redeploy retained staff to high-priority areas; enforce hiring moratorium in areas with surplus; track payroll savings monthly

diagnostic_signals.signal: Employee cost as % of revenue >35% vs manufacturing/utility sector benchmark of <25%
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Manpower rationalisation will materially improve operating leverage

diagnostic_signals.signal: Average employee age >45 with >40% workforce eligible for retirement in <10 years creating transition opportunity
diagnostic_signals.confirms: VRS and natural attrition can reduce headcount without retrenchment

diagnostic_signals.signal: Employee-to-revenue ratio 2–3× peer PSU benchmark for similar business scale
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Productivity improvement through rationalisation is significant

required_data_fields: Workforce headcount by function, grade, age, and location
Payroll cost breakdown by grade and allowance type
DPE benchmark: employee cost ratios for comparable CPSEs
VRS eligibility rules and DPE approval requirements
5-year payroll forecast with and without rationalisation

condition_precedents: Project Management Office (PMO) established with Earned Value Management (EVM)
Contractor governance framework with performance bonds in place
DPR (Detailed Project Report) quality gate implemented before sanction
industry_sources: CAG — Audit Reports on Infrastructure Projects (cag.gov.in/en/report) — project cost overrun patterns in Indian CPSEs
DPE — MoU Performance Evaluation of CPSEs 2023 (dpe.gov.in) — project delivery KPIs
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Confirm scope for Capital Project Cost & Overrun Control: validate baseline and stakeholders
Design target process and RACI
Pilot in one BU/geo and measure effort/cost delta
Scale rollout and update SOPs/training

diagnostic_signals.signal: High FTE effort or cycle time vs benchmark
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Process inefficiency is material

required_data_fields: Process volume metrics
FTE effort or cost allocation by process
Baseline cycle times

source_verification_note: industry_sources entries indicate the published works that directionally support each lever's savings thesis. Where no external benchmark was found, INTERNAL_CALIBRATION is noted. All cited sources are real publications; specific savings percentages remain internal calibrations unless the source directly cites a matching range.
